content: Just as the Tree of Thoughts (ToT) framework for large language models structures deliberate problem-solving by growing and pruning branches of candidate reasoning paths, the human brain’s active memory mechanisms grow new synaptic connections and prune weak inactive synapses based on curiosity-driven exploration of the environment. We hypothesize that pruning low-curiosity reasoning branches in Tree of Thoughts architectures, modeled after how active memory forgets uninformative weak memory traces, will improve open-ended problem-solving efficiency in autonomous agents.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
